title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing othersâ€™ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported project leads, Kalyn Dorheim /Jianqiu Zheng. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. Community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident. code conduct, including consequences laid , applies equally community leaders, course. temporary permanent ban result person question removed leadership.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.0, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/0/ code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired Mozillaâ€™s code conduct enforcement ladder. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https:// www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Compare Model Configurations
code: library(MEMC)     # MEMC should already be installed, see installation instructions.  library(ggplot2)  # package used to visualize results  library(knitr)    # makes nice tables library(magrittr) # import the %>% pipeline  # set a theme to use in all the plots theme_set(theme_bw() + theme(legend.title = element_blank()))

title: Compare Model Configurations
text: three models configurations included MEMC, MEND_model, COMISSION_model, CORPSE_model. models built using parameter tables initial starting conditions, differ represent DOM POM decomposition well MB decay. Compare model configurations. Visualize outputs.
code: knitr::kable(rbind(MEND_model$table, COMISSION_model$table, CORPSE_model$table)) # Set up the time vector  time <- seq(from = 1, to = 3000, by = 10)  # Solve model the Men mend_out <- solve_model(MEND_model, time = time) comiss_out <- solve_model(COMISSION_model, time = time) corpse_out <- solve_model(CORPSE_model, time = time)  out <- rbind(mend_out, comiss_out, corpse_out) out %>%    ggplot(aes(time, value, color = name, linetype = name)) +    geom_line(size = 0.75) +    facet_wrap("variable", scales = "free") +    labs(title = "Model Configurations", y = unique(out$units)) +    facet_wrap("variable", scales = "free") +    theme(legend.title = element_blank())

what: Fit MEND model to experimental data
title: Fit to Data
text: memc contains function called memc_modfit helps users fit model run comparison data. Run model best parameter fits.
code: # Update the MEND model initial state conditions to reflect the experimental set up. MEND_model$state <- c(P=4.71, M=17.67, Q=0, B=0.82, D=0.148,  EP=0.0082, EM=0.0082, IC=0, Tot=23.484)  # Fit the the "maximum specific decomposition" parameters.  mend_fit <- memc_modfit(config = MEND_model, comp_data = comp_data, x = c(V.d=2, V.p=5, V.m=0.01)) mend_out <- solve_model(MEND_model,  0:max(comp_data$time), params = mend_fit$par)  mend_out %>%    filter(variable == "IC") %>%    ggplot() +    geom_line(aes(time, value)) +    geom_point(data = comp_data, aes(time, IC)) +    labs(title = "Fitted MEMC IC vs Experimental Data",         y =  "mg C/g soil")

what: Fit COMISSION model to experimental data
title: Fit to Data
text: Run model best parameter fits.
code: # Update the COMISSSION model initial state conditions to reflect the experimental set up. COMISSION_model$state <- c(P=4.71, M=17.67, Q=0, B=0.82, D=0.148,  EP=0.0082, EM=0.0082, IC=0, Tot=23.484)  # Fit the the "maximum specific decomposition" parameters.  comission_fit <- memc_modfit(config = COMISSION_model, comp_data = comp_data, x =c(V.d=2, V.p=5, V.m=0.01)) comission_out <- solve_model(COMISSION_model,  0:max(comp_data$time), params = comission_fit$par)  comission_out %>%    filter(variable == "IC") %>%    ggplot() +    geom_line(aes(time, value)) +    geom_point(data = comp_data, aes(time, IC)) +    labs(title = "Fitted MEMC IC vs Experimental Data",         y =  "mg C/g soil")

what: Compare the different model configurations and fits
title: Fit to Data
text: different fitted parameter values two model configurations? different IC output?
code: bind_rows(mend_out, comission_out) %>%    filter(variable == "IC") %>%    ggplot() +    geom_line(aes(time, value, color = name, linetype = name), size = 1) +    geom_point(data = comp_data, aes(time, IC)) +    labs(title = "Fitted MEMC IC vs Experimental Data",         y =  "mg C/g soil") #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> â„ą Please use `linewidth` instead. #> This warning is displayed once every 8 hours. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated. bind_rows(mend_out, comission_out) %>%    ggplot() +    geom_line(aes(time, value, color = name, linetype = name), size = 1) +    labs(title = "Comparing Two Fitted MEMC Models",         y =  "mg C/g soil") +    facet_wrap("variable", scales = "free")

title: Getting Started
text: * TODO add text different pools & flux names * TODO feedback JZ strucutre/names

title: Getting Started
text: Start building default MEMC model configuration, using default parameter values initial state conditions. mod object created configure_model information model configuration name, table defining kinetics used, parameters, initial state conditions model configuration. Solve model various time steps. Visualize run results.
code: library(MEMC)  library(ggplot2) # package used to visualize results theme_set(theme_bw() + theme(legend.title = element_blank())) mod <- configure_model(params = default_params, state = default_initial, name = "Default") time <- seq(0, 36500, by=25)  out1 <- solve_model(mod, time) ggplot(data = out1) +    geom_line(aes(time, value, color = name), size = 0.75) +    facet_wrap("variable", scales = "free") +    labs(y = "mg C/g soil",         title = "Default MEMC Run") #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> â„ą Please use `linewidth` instead. #> This warning is displayed once every 8 hours. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated.

title: Getting Started
code: # Double the size of the biomass pool new_initial_cond <- default_initial new_initial_cond[["B"]] <- new_initial_cond[["B"]] * 2  # You can either configure a new model using the new initial conditions or pass the  # new initial conditions to the model configuration using the solve_model function. out2 <- solve_model(mod, time, state = new_initial_cond)  # Rename the output run.  out2$name <- "Double B" to_plot <- rbind(out1, out2)   ggplot(to_plot) +   geom_line(aes(time, value, color = name, linetype = name), size = 0.75) +    facet_wrap("variable", scales = "free") +    labs(y = "mg C/g soil",         title = "Change Initial State Conditions")

title: Getting Started
text: Pass new parameter value. Visualize model runs.
code: out3 <- solve_model(mod, time, params = c("V.d" = 10)) out3 $name <- "Increase V.d" to_plot <- rbind(out1, out3)   ggplot(to_plot) +   geom_line(aes(time, value, color = name, linetype = name), size = 0.75) +    facet_wrap("variable", scales = "free") +    labs(y = "mg C/g soil",         title = "Change V.d")

title: Install & Build
text: Follow download installation instructions R R studio. Use remotes install MEMC built R package directory github.
code: # use install.packages("remotes") to install this package the first time. library(remotes)  # Now build and install the R package on your local machine. install_github('Microbial-Explicit-Model/MEMC')   # You can also install from specific git tags... install_github("Microbial-Explicit-Model/MEMC@version")  # ...branches... install_github("Microbial-Explicit-Model/MEMC@branch_name")  # ...or commit hashes. install_github("Microbial-Explicit-Model/MEMC@commit-hash")

title: Install & Build
text: Clone repository github install package development mode. Build package calling devtools::load_all() R root directory. Please see r-pkgs information build develop R packages. making changes code, checking new git branch, pulling new commits need clean rebuild MEMC package. Rstudio click Build > Clean Rebuild run following R console.
code: devtools::build()

title: Senstivity Analysis
text: using memc_sensrange based FME::sensRange run two different model configurations. Start setting parameters vary. Set data frame defining parameter ranges sample . Define time vector solve model . Use memc_sensrange complete sensitivity analysis MEND model.  Run sentivity analysis COMISSION model configuration.  Compare output two models.
code: pars <- c("V.d" = 3.0e+00,"V.p" = 1.4e+01,"V.m" = 2.5e-01) prange <- data.frame(min = pars - pars * 0.75,                       max = pars + pars * 0.75) time <- seq(0, 365) MENDsens_out <- memc_sensrange(config = MEND_model, t = time,                                 pars = pars, parRange = prange, dist = "latin", n = 50) mend_to_plot <- format_sensout(MENDsens_out) mend_to_plot$name <- "MEND" ggplot(data = mend_to_plot) +    geom_ribbon(aes(time, ymin = q25, ymax = q75, fill = "q25/q75"), alpha = 0.5) +    geom_line(aes(time, Mean)) +    facet_wrap("variable", scales = "free") +    labs(y = "mg C/g soil",         title = "MEND V.d, V.p, V.m Sensitivity") +      theme(legend.title = element_blank()) COMISSIONsens_out <- memc_sensrange(config = COMISSION_model, t = time,                                 pars = pars, parRange = prange, dist = "latin", n = 50) comission_to_plot <- format_sensout(COMISSIONsens_out) comission_to_plot$name <- "COMISSION" ggplot(data = comission_to_plot) +    geom_ribbon(aes(time, ymin = q25, ymax = q75, fill = "q25/q75"), alpha = 0.5) +    geom_line(aes(time, Mean)) +    facet_wrap("variable", scales = "free") +    labs(y = "mg C/g soil",         title = "COMISSION V.d, V.p, V.m Sensitivity") +    theme(legend.title = element_blank()) to_plot <- rbind(comission_to_plot, mend_to_plot) to_plot <- to_plot[to_plot$variable %in% c("P", "M", "Tot"), ]  ggplot(data = to_plot) +    geom_ribbon(aes(time, ymin = q25, ymax = q75, fill = name), alpha = 0.4) +    geom_line(aes(time, Mean, linetype = name)) +    facet_wrap("variable", scales = "free") +    labs(y = "mg C/g soil",         title = "Sensitivity to V.d, V.p, V.m") +    theme(legend.title = element_blank())

title: Senstivity Analysis
text: Test local effect parameters model output memc_sensfun, based FME::sensFun. Select parameters test, use parameters used global sensitivity analysis. Run local sensitivity analysis. Format output dataframe easy plot.
code: pars <- c("V.d" = 3.0e+00,"V.p" = 1.4e+01,"V.m" = 2.5e-01) out <- memc_sensfunc(config = MEND_model, t = time, pars = pars) to_plot <- format_sensout(out) ggplot(data = to_plot) +   geom_line(aes(time, value, color = parameter)) +   facet_wrap("variable", scales = "free") +    labs(y = NULL, x = NULL, title = "Local Sensitivity")

title: Microbial Explicit Model Comparison: a R package supporting model exploration
text: MEMC R package representation different microbial explicit carbon models flexible framework model comparison exercises.

title: Microbial Explicit Model Comparison: a R package supporting model exploration
text: Follow download installation instructions R R studio. Use remotes install MEMC built R package directory github.
code: # use install.packages("remotes") to install this package the first time. library(remotes)  # Now build and install the R package on your local machine. install_github('Microbial-Explicit-Model/MEMC')

title: MEMC model configurations for MEND Wang et al. 2013 â€” COMISSION_model

code: COMISSION_model

title: MEMC model configurations for MEND Wang et al. 2013 â€” COMISSION_model
text: object created form configure_model. name COMISSION, model name table table describing user selected kinetics params default MEMC parameter table state default inital state values MEMC

what: Set up a model configuration â€” configure_model
title: Set up a model configuration â€” configure_model
text: Set model configuration

title: Set up a model configuration â€” configure_model
code: configure_model(   params,   state,   name = "unnamed",   DOMdecomp = "MM",   POMdecomp = "MM",   MBdecay = "DD" )

title: Set up a model configuration â€” configure_model
text: params data.table containing following columns: parameter, value, units. state vector initial state values, must named name string name model configuration, default set "MEND". DOMdecomp string indicating dynamics used model microbial decomposition DOM, one  following "MM", "RMM", "ECA" POMdecomp string indicating dynamics used model microbial decomposition POM, one  following "MM", "RMM", "ECA", "LM" MBdecay string indicating microbial decay, one  following ""LM" "DD"

title: The default inital carbon pool values the basic MEMC model configurations from Wang et al. 2013 â€” default_initial
text: vector intial states Carbon pools MEND 2013 Wang et al. 2013. 'P' 'Particulate organic carbon POC' 'M' 'Mineral associated organic carbon MOC, note excludes Q' 'Q' 'Adsorbed phase DOC' 'B' 'Microbial biomass carbon MBC' 'D' 'Dissolved organic carbon DOC' 'EP' 'Enzyme decomposition P' 'EM' 'Ensyme decomposition M' 'IC' 'Accumulated CO2 flux' 'Tot' 'Total carbon patch'

what: Identifiers for MEMC supported flux representations â€” dynamics
title: Identifiers for MEMC supported flux representations â€” dynamics
text: identifiers correspond various dynamics users can select  configure_model

title: Identifiers for MEMC supported flux representations â€” dynamics
text: MM Michaelisâ€“Menten kineticsf RMM Reverse Michaelis-Menten kinetics, (Schimel Weintraub, 2003) (Sulman et al. 2014) ECA Equilibrium chemistry approximation, based RESOM (Tang Riley, 2014) DD Density dependent microbial decay LM Linear model, (Robertson et al. 2019)

title: Identifiers for MEMC supported flux representations â€” dynamics
text: Schimel, J. P., M. N. Weintraub (2003), implications exoenzyme activity microbial carbon nitrogen limitation soil: theoretical model, Soil Biol. Biochem., 35(4), 549â€“563, doi:10.1016/s0038-0717(03)00015-4 Sulman, B. N., R. P. Phillips, . C. Oishi, E. Shevliakova, S. W. Pacala (2014), Microbe-driven turnover offsets mineral-mediated storage soil carbon elevated CO2, Nat. Clim. Change, 4(12), 1099â€“1102, doi:10.1038/nclimate2436. Tang, J., Riley, W. Weaker soil carbonâ€“climate feedbacks resulting microbial abiotic interactions. Nature Clim Change 5, 56â€“60 (2015). https://doi.org/10.1038/nclimate2438 Robertson, . D., Paustian, K., Ogle, S., Wallenstein, M. D., Lugato, E., Cotrufo, M. F.: Unifying soil organic matter formation persistence frameworks: MEMS model, Biogeosciences, 16, 1225â€“1248, https://doi.org/10.5194/bg-16-1225-2019, 2019.

title: Format the output returned by memc_sensrange or memc_sensfunc into long data format for easy plotting â€” format_sensout
text: long dataframe summary memc_sensrange

title: Format the output returned by memc_sensrange or memc_sensfunc into long data format for easy plotting â€” format_sensout
code: if (FALSE) { # Test the sensitivity of the MEND output for V.p, K.p, V.m, K.m, V.d, K.d pars <- c("V.d" = 3.0e+00,"V.p" = 1.4e+01,"V.m" = 2.5e-01) prange <- data.frame(min = pars - pars * 0.75, max = pars + pars * 0.75) t <- floor(seq(0, 365, length.out = 10)) out <- format_sensout(config = MEND_model, t = t, pars = pars, parRange = prange, dist = "latin", num = 10) to_plot <- format_sensout(out) ggplot(data = to_plot) +    geom_line(aes(time, Mean)) +    geom_ribbon(aes(time, ymin = Min, ymax = Max), alpha = 0.5) +    facet_wrap("variable", scales = "free") }

title: Estimate the local effect of a parameter on a memc model output â€” memc_sensfunc
code: if (FALSE) { # Test the sensitivity of the MEND output for V.p, K.p, V.m pars <- c("V.d" = 3.0e+00,"V.p" = 1.4e+01,"V.m" = 2.5e-01) out <- memc_sensfunc(config = MEND_model, t = t, pars = pars) pairs(out) plot(out) # Using the helper functions to make nic ggplots to_plot <- format_sensout(out) ggplot(data = to_plot) +    geom_line(aes(time, value, color = parameter)) +    facet_wrap("variable", scales = "free") }

title: Run the memc model with the FME sensRange â€” memc_sensrange
code: if (FALSE) { # Test the sensitivity of the MEND output for V.p, K.p, V.m pars <- c("V.d" = 3.0e+00,"V.p" = 1.4e+01,"V.m" = 2.5e-01) prange <- data.frame(min = pars - pars * 0.75, max = pars + pars * 0.75) t <- floor(seq(0, 365, length.out = 10)) out <- memc_sensrange(config = MEND_model, t = t, pars = pars, parRange = prange, dist = "latin", num = 10) plot(summary(out)) # Using the helper functions. to_plot <- format_sensout(out) ggplot(data = to_plot) +    geom_line(aes(time, Mean)) +    geom_ribbon(aes(time, ymin = Min, ymax = Max), alpha = 0.5) +    facet_wrap("variable", scales = "free") }
